document.title: IEC 60601-1 Test Report with IEC 60601-1-6 Annex A and National Differences Attachments
document.revisionOrVersion: Report No. ACTS-2025-SM-261; Annex A Report No. ACTS-2025-SM-261_Annex A

sourceSummary.oneLine: Final chunk of ACTS-2025-SM-261 contains IEC 62366-1 usability checklist completion, Korea and US national differences, and supporting attachments for photos, user manual, diagrams, schematics, and PCB patterns.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports regulatory submission evidence for IEC 60601-1 electrical safety, usability engineering, national deviation review, and technical construction documentation.
keyPoints: IEC 62366-1 usability engineering checklist items for user interface specification and evaluation planning are marked pass.
Korea national differences cite MFDS Notification No. 2020-12 Annex 1 and include voltage, frequency, Korean-language instruction, and plug requirements.
US national differences cite AAMI ES60601-1 editions and IECEE OD-2020-F3:2022, with many X-ray, permanently installed equipment, and gas-cylinder clauses marked not applicable.
Report attachments include photographs, user manual, dimension diagram, schematics diagram, and PCB pattern diagram.

entities.evidence: Summative evaluation rows reference Usability Summative Evaluation Plan Doc. No. A-USEP-CYN, Rev.0 for data analysis and data collection methods.

entities.evidence: Some summative evaluation rows reference Usability Summative Evaluation Plan Doc. No. A-USEP-BLU, Rev.0.

concepts.name: IEC 62366-1 usability engineering process checklist
concepts.evidence: Annex I is titled IEC 62366-1:2015, AMD1:2020 - Usability engineering process checklist.

concepts.name: User interface specification
concepts.evidence: Table 5.6 covers usability engineering file results for user interface specification with pass verdicts.

concepts.name: User interface evaluation plan
concepts.evidence: Table 5.7 covers usability engineering file results for user interface evaluation plan with pass verdicts.

concepts.name: Korea national differences for IEC 60601-1
concepts.evidence: Attachment 1 is titled National Differences for Korea and cites Republic of Korea national differences.

concepts.name: US national differences for IEC 60601-1
concepts.evidence: Attachment 2 is titled National Differences for US and cites AAMI ES60601-1 national standard differences.

concepts.name: Technical construction attachments
concepts.evidence: Attachments include photographs, user manual, dimension diagram, schematics diagram, and PCB pattern diagram.

evidence.claim: The Annex A usability checklist applies IEC 62366-1:2015 with AMD1:2020.
evidence.evidenceText: Annex I - IEC 62366-1:2015, AMD1:2020 - Usability engineering process checklist.

evidence.claim: The user interface specification checklist was assessed as pass using Usability Engineering File A-UEF-CYN Rev.0.
evidence.evidenceText: Table 5.6 User Interface Specification references Usability Engineering File Document No. A-UEF-CYN, Rev.0; rows for usability specification, use specification, hazards, risk controls, documentation, and training show verdict P.

evidence.claim: The manufacturer established and maintained a user interface evaluation plan, with evidence in Usability Validation Plan A-UEP-CYN Rev.0 section 4 Test Plan.
evidence.evidenceText: Requirement states manufacturer shall establish and maintain a User Interface Evaluation plan; result references Usability Validation Plan Doc. No. A-UEP-CYN, Rev.0, 4 Test Plan; verdict P.

evidence.claim: Formative evaluation planning addressed test method, criteria, and data collection timing.
evidence.evidenceText: Formative evaluation rows reference Usability Formative Evaluation Plan Doc. No. A-UFEP-CYN, Rev.0 sections 2.1 Test Method, 2.2 Test Criteria, and 6 Data Collection Method with verdict P.

evidence.claim: Summative evaluation planning addressed objective evidence, evaluated user interface parts, safety information criteria, training/document availability, participant representativeness, user groups, environment, correct use, and use-error data collection.
evidence.evidenceText: Summative evaluation rows reference A-USEP-CYN Rev.0 and A-USEP-BLU Rev.0 sections for Data Analysis Method, Data Collection Method, Practitioner Script, Participant, Test environment, Usage scenarios related to risk factors, all with verdict P.

evidence.claim: Korea national differences are based on MFDS Notification No. 2020-12 Annex 1.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachment 1 Republic of Korea National Differences states Differences according to: Notification of MFDS No. 2020-12, Annex 1.

evidence.claim: For Korea, the certified POE injector is rated 100-240 Vac and includes a 50-60 Hz frequency range including 60 Hz.
evidence.evidenceText: Korea national differences list Certified POE injector: 100-240 Vac and POE injector: 50-60 Hz; verdict P.

evidence.claim: For Korea, safety-related instruction manuals and appliance markings must be Korean or IEC 417 graphical symbols, and Korean is to be used when supplied to Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: Instruction row states manuals and safety markings including nameplate shall be in Korean or graphical symbols per IEC Publication 417; result says Korean is to be used when equipment is supplied to Korea; verdict P.

evidence.claim: For Korea, mains plugs must comply with Korean Standards KSC 8305 and 8300.
evidence.evidenceText: Plug row states plugs for connection to supply mains shall comply with Korean Standard KSC 8305 and 8300; result refers to appended Table 8.10; verdict P.

evidence.claim: US national differences apply AAMI ES60601-1:2005 and amendments AMD1:2012 and AMD2:2021 using IECEE OD-2020-F3:2022 Ed. 1.2.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachment 2 states Differences according to national standard AAMI ES60601-1:2005, AAMI ES60601-1:2005/AMD1:2012, AAMI ES60601-1:2005/AMD2:2021; TRF template used IECEE OD-2020-F3:2022 Ed. 1.2.

evidence.claim: Under US national differences, IEC 60601-1 clause 8.7.3 allowable earth leakage current values were considered and passed.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 8.7.3 allowable values states earth leakage current allowable values are 5 mA in normal condition and 10 mA in single fault condition; result Considered; verdict P.

evidence.claim: US national differences mark X-ray-specific requirements as not applicable because the equipment is not an X-ray system or X-ray ME equipment.
evidence.evidenceText: Rows for X-ray mode of operation, marking, protective earthing, hospital-grade plug current rating, and related exceptions state Not X-Ray system or Not X-ray ME equipment with verdict N/A.

evidence.claim: The test report includes photographic evidence of external, cable terminal, internal, board, LAN, SW, and POE injector views.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachment 3 Photographs lists external views with optional handle, cable terminal view, internal view, main board, gate board, AED board, LAN board, SW board, and POE injector views.

evidence.claim: The test report includes supporting attachments for user manual, dimension diagram, schematics diagram, and PCB pattern diagram.
evidence.evidenceText: Attachment 4 User Manual spans 36 pages; Attachment 5 Dimension diagram has 1 page; Attachment 6 Schematics Diagram spans 18 pages; Attachment 7 PCB Pattern Diagram spans 27 pages.

reviewFlags.reason: Chunk text is PDF-encoded/mojibake, but repeated report labels, standards, document numbers, and verdicts are recoverable from context.
Some summative evaluation references use A-USEP-BLU Rev.0 instead of A-USEP-CYN Rev.0, which may indicate a template carryover or cross-product document reference requiring reviewer confirmation.
This is chunk 3 of 3; document title, product model, and date details may be more fully supported in earlier chunks.
